material.mattext: Which of the following is an example of physical evidence?
What type of evidence is most valuable in proving that a criminal culprit is guilty of a crime?
The choice that contains an example of identified evidence followed by an example of individualized evidence is
Physical evidence is important to a criminal investigation because it can
An example of an identified piece of physical evidence is
An example of an individualized piece of physical evidence is
An example of a substance in which a visible fingerprint impression could be found is
You must know which hand a fingerprint came from if you want to identify which type of fingerprint ridge pattern?
What are the three basic types of fingerprint ridge patterns?
The two fingerprint patterns from left to right are
Which fingerprint enhancement technique should be used to enhance any fingerprints on this piece of crime scene evidence?
Fingerprint lifting powder is applied to a surface by
Cryanoacrylate fumigation might be effective in the enhancement of latent fingerprints on a
Iodine fumigation is effective at enhancing any latent fingerprints on a
The two fingerprint patterns from left to right are
The reason latent fingerprints must be enhanced is that they
are not visible to the human eye
The application of fingerprint lifting powder is effective in the enhancement of any latent fingerprints on a
According to the Locard Principle, it is not possible to come into contact with an environment without
adding to it or taking parts of it away
Two investigative tools used to collect and analyze forensic trace evidence are
The first three tasks in the initial investigation of a crime scene are
analysis of evidence, collection of evidence, and search warrant
arrest or suspect, prosecution of culprit, and conviction of criminal
securing the crime scene, make visual observations, and collection of evidence
analysis of crime scene, examination of evidence, and isolation of crime scene
The choice that contains an example of organic evidence followed by an example of inorganic evidence is
Which of the following types of crime scene evidence is organic?
Hair evidence can be useful in a forensic investigation because it
might contain traces of DNA and remains intact for a long period of time
May have traces of alcohol in it and requires less time for analysis
does not have any living cells and is simple to duplicate
always contains mitochondrial DNA and is inexpensive to test
What conclusion can be drawn about this hair root?
What does the hair tip indicate about the armed robbery suspect?
his hair has not been cut for a long time
The shape of a human hair root that has fallen out naturally is
The choice that correctly identifies the type of hair medulla pictured and the organism from which it came is
What does the hair tip indicate about the arson suspect?
His hair has not been cut for over a year
His hair has beenexposed to heat and flames
To collect immediately any foreign fibres that may be related to the crime from a victim or suspect is important because they can
Fibre evidence is often found at crime scenes where there has been
a verbal threat made by the suspect
plastic explosives left at the crime scene
physical contact between the suspect and the victim
An inorganic fibre at a crime scene is a positive find because many inorganic fibres are found in
lighter shades and often only made during certain seasons
unique shapes and often only produced for a short time
that a theft of property occurred and the victim lost money
physical contact was made and the type of contact made
the number of suspects and victims involved
a violent crime happened and that the victim was injured
A natural fibre that is helpful to find in a criminal investigation because it is rare is
The souce of the blood in this micrograph is likely a

After analysis of blood from a crime scene, it is determined that the blood type of an unknown sexual assault culprit is AB+.

How would this information be useful to police investigators?
It could directly identify the guilty culprit because no two blood types are the same.
It would assist the victim in describing the appearance of the assailant.
It could eliminate some suspects because it is the least common blood type.
It would help specify the age and gender of the offender
If a person is a secretor, a substance from his or her body, other than blood, that could be tested to determine his or her blood type is
What happens when a force from a fist hits an exposed source of blood?
A large amount of cast off residue drops onto the victim.
Many small droplets of blood spray from the wound.
A few large droplets of blood free fall from the injury.
A circular blood stain is left upon the fist.
What test or analysis can be performed on clothing to determine if it has any semen stains upon it?
The blood spatter pattern shown was likely created by blood
spraying onto a wall from a victim beaten by a baseball bat
dropping onto a floor from an open wound
spreading onto the ground from a victim hit by a bus
hitting a surface from a gunshot wound
spreading onto the ground from a victim hit by a bus
After blood evidence from a crime scene has been confirmed to be from a human, what type of test should be done to eliminate potential suspects?
Identifying one type of blood enzyme or protein within a blood evidence sample assists investigators because it
determines the type of crime that occurred
helps to narrow down the list of suspects
established the exact time of the crime
If a circular droplet of blood on the floor is smudged due to movement after it has partially dried, then
a peripheral ring stain will be left behind
What test could investigators perform at the crime scene to confirm their suspicions that the specks are from blood?
Investigators believe that blood from the victim was cleaned up in the bathroom after the murder. Investigators could prove this suspicion by performing a
A behaviour caused by alcohol consumption that impairs a person's ability to drive a motor vehicle is
A person is considered to be legally impaired in Canada if he or she
has more than 80 mg of alcohol per 100 ml of blood
drinks two cans of beer in two hours
drinks one glass of wine in three hours
has more than 50mg of alcohol in 100mL of blood
The organ in the body that breaks down alcohol into less harmful substances is the
The part of the brain that is most sensitive to alcohol is the
The choice that correctly describes the Breathalyzer device and the Intoxilyzer device is
Determines how much alcohol is absorbed by the live, Analyzes how much alcohol has affected the brain
Causes alcohol to ferment, Causes the breakdown of alcohol
Converts alcohol into water, Absorbs alcohol into a vacuum
Chemical reaction produces a color change, Identifies molecules as they absorb infrared light
Why has Intoxilyzer testing become more popular than Breathalyzer testing with law enforcement agencies?
It is more portable and can identify the type of alcohol consumed.
It does not require an operator and can detect the use of drugs.
It is less expensive and does not ever require any repairs.
It does not require any chemicals and has a lower risk of operator error.
Which of the following yields alcohol over time?
Which type of beverage has the highest concentration of alcohol per cup?
How does the concentration of alcohol in the blood compare with the concentration of alcohol in the alveoli?
Alcohol is broken down into acetic acid in the alveoli.
Alcohol is converted into sulfuric acid by the liver.
The alcohol concentration is the same in both.
The alcohol concentration is 2100 times greater in the blood.
What type of sample is analyzed by the Intoxilyzer instrument?
The type of technology the Intoxilyzer uses to analyze an alcohol breath sample is
The system that controls the body's involuntary actions during the stress of lying is
The four types of physiological data that are collected by the modern polygraph instrument are
amount of urine, blood pressure, glucose secretion, use of language
blood pressure data, breathing rate, heart rate, levels of perspiration
eye movements, breathing rate, hearing, levels of perspiration
glucagon levels, digestive activities, heart rate, reflexes

The polygraphist asks a homicide suspect a mixture of relevant questions and control questions. Some questions are directly related to the murder while others are similar in nature to the crime but not directly related to the case. The suspect is not told there is a difference between the relevant and control questions. What kind of questioning technique is being used?
The subject likely lied when she answered questions
If quick movements are necessary during stress, the body directs a large amount of blood towards the
What is a phrase that has been coined for the response that occurs during stress?
A polygraph examination is an effective criminal investigative tool used to
question a suspect's knowledge of crime scene details
interrogate a suspect in the hopes of getting a confession
understand a suspect's motive for committing the crime
argue whether a suspect's alibi is accurate
The slope of handwritten text is the most noticeable when
A technique used to individualize the ink used in a suspicious document is
The choice that correctly describes a forensic writing sample and the number of writing samples taken from one criminal subject is
Subject is given a series of random words. Seven
Subject is told what to write. Three
The letters in a deliberately forged document are often written
A letter that likely will be easy to individualize is
The two types of DNA fragments used in DNA profiling are
What information can be interpreted from these DNA profile examples?
Suspect B and the culprit are related to each other.
Suspect A's DNA profile matches the culprit's DNA profile
Suspect A and suspect B's DNA matches the culprit's DNA.
Suspect B's DNA profile matches the culprit's DNA profile.
Analysis of mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) is useful in some forensic investigations because
each person has the same mtDNA as their father
each person has the same mtDNA as their mother
it is identical in all human males
it is identical in all human females
In forensic investigations, why is the PCR method of DNA analysis used more often than the RFLP method?
It is portable and can be done at the crime scene.
It is less complicated and more environmentally friendly.
It is faster and requires fewer cells for analysis
It is less expensive and does not yield any negative matches
Individual DNA bands can be seen readily after they have been placed in the gel electrophoresis device because they are
genes needed for the production of thymine
unique sections of DNA found in repetitive patterns
distinct sequences of DNA found only in human females
genes responsible for the creation of proteins
The best way to try and individualize the bones of human remains is through
The probability ratio that is included with every DNA match indicates
the occurrence of any laboratory interpretation errors
the numerical chance the DNA profiled could belong to someone else
the accuracy of the match as compared to other matches
the number of STR sequences that matched the crime scene sample
If you were jury member at a criminal trial, what probability ratio related to a DNA profile match would you find the most convincing?
Which of the following individuals have their DNA profile registered in Canada's national DNA data bank?
A criminal convicted of first degree murder
A criminal suspect arrested for impaired driving
A body fluid that DNA could likely be extracted from is
What device is used to separate DNA fragments during the creation of a DNA profile?
The DNA analysis method used in most forensic criminal investigations is
What is unique about STR and VNTR fragments of DNA?
They are responsible for making thousands of different proteins.
They are the same in all family members.
They are only passed on from a mother to her offspring
They are individually distinctive sequences found in repetitive patterns.
The family members from this pedigree that have the same mitochondrial DNA as Jane are
What is applied to STR and VNTR sequences to make them more visible in a DNA profile?
How many loci are examined when a forensic DNA match is being attempted?
